doc: Voice patch for strum-harp.json (ADR-0032). One monophonic plucked-string voice: a held freq tunes the modal resonator's fundamental, a held gate fires its internal mallet (the resonator's own attack + decay ring, no separate envelope/VCA needed). No `active` output: the voice pool falls back to keying liveness on `gate`, and because a stolen voice reuses the same resonator instance (not a fresh spawn), its ring simply continues underneath a re-trigger rather than cutting off. NO filter here — the brightness Good Button drives a single master filter on the summed mix (host post-mix). interface: freq/gate in, audio out.

nodes.doc: Plucked string: tuned by the voice's freq, pinged by the voice's gate (rising edge fires the internal mallet — no external `in` excitation wired). Macro controls (structure/brightness/damping/position) are left at their operator defaults, which read as a mildly-stretched, moderately-bright, moderately-long ring plucked near the string's end.
